summary: The Reverser pe-constructor-installs analyzer now supports owner-register filtering and opt-in de-duplication, then the upgraded workflow was run against the 947 bootstrap/config setup range. Filtering to owner register RDI and de-duplicating same constructor/store endpoints produced 11 unique client-owned constructor installs from the earlier broad native scan.

new_cli_flags: --owner-register REG, repeatable, filters installs by the recovered owner/base register
--dedupe-installs, suppresses duplicate candidates that converge on the same constructor call and final slot store
tool_update.why_it_was_added: The prior broad bootstrap scan exposed two repeatable workflow needs: suppress nested non-client stores such as R14+0x10 and collapse overlapping allocator windows that reached the same constructor/store endpoint at client+0x199B0.

unique_client_slot_worklist.note: The dedupe key kept the first overlapping candidate and suppressed the second same constructor/store endpoint. Follow-up semantic typing should still inspect the nearby 0xF8 allocation at 0x140021FBF.

relationship_to_prior_findings: Preserves the previous broad scan as raw evidence while adding a cleaner reproducible client-owned view.
Confirms that the R14+0x10 row from the broad scan is not part of the RDI-owned client-slot lattice.
Collapses the duplicate client+0x199B0 endpoint into one worklist row, making future constructor/literal/use-site enrichment less noisy.
Keeps client+0x198D0 as the known login/auth controller anchor inside a larger bootstrap object cluster.
opennxt_947_server_implications: The OpenNXT 947 startup parity worklist is now a concrete set of 11 unique client-owned bootstrap services/controllers instead of a noisier 13-row native candidate list.
Future server/client comparison can prioritize semantic naming of slots around the known auth controller: client+0x198C0, client+0x198E0, client+0x198E8, and client+0x19900.
The cleaned owner-filtered map is a better base for correlating JS5, login-key, network/bootstrap, scheduler, cache/archive, and session-root consumers.
verification: JSON parse validation for the filtered native artifact and summary
next_targets: Use pe-function-literals and pe-function-calls on the 11 constructors to attach semantic hints.
Run pe-field-refs for the 11 slot offsets with --base-register RDI to map consumers and destructor/update paths.
Consider a future compact summary exporter for pe-constructor-installs so this filtered worklist can be generated automatically.
